reviewTexts:
[2024-12-11] La Pilareta in Valencia truly stands out as a gem in the culinary landscape. From the moment you step into this charming eatery, you're enveloped by an authentic Mediterranean vibe that promises a memorable dining experience.

The fried calamares here are nothing short of spectacular. They strike the perfect balance between crispy and tender, making each bite a delight. Similarly, the Grilled Cuttlefish is a testament to the kitchen's mastery over seafood, grilled to perfection with just the right char and a smoky flavor that complements the natural sweetness of the cuttlefish.

Don't miss out on their potatoes; they're a simple dish done exceptionally well - crispy on the outside, soft and flavorful inside. The mussels are another highlight, fresh, succulent, and served with a preparation that enhances their natural briny taste without overpowering it.

The atmosphere at La Pilareta matches the quality of the food. It's unpretentious yet inviting, reflecting the vibrant yet relaxed spirit of Valencia. The service is warm and attentive, adding to the overall experience, making you feel like you're dining with friends.

If you're planning a revisit, you're in for another treat as the menu, while rooted in traditional Valencian cuisine, always seems to have something new or a seasonal twist that keeps dining here exciting. La Pilareta isn't just a meal; it's an experience that beckons you to come back for more. Highly recommended for anyone looking to savor the essence of Valencian gastronomy.

[2025-12-08] La Pilareta – A Classic Valencian Experience with Flavor and Charm

La Pilareta is one of Valencia’s most iconic spots, and from the moment you step inside, you feel it. The welcoming atmosphere makes you instantly comfortable, almost like you’ve walked into a beloved local institution that knows how to treat its guests right.

The vintage decoration adds so much character—old wood , classic tiles, traditional touches, and a sense of history that gives the place its unmistakable charm. It feels authentic, warm, and full of Valencian soul.

But the real highlight is the food. La Pilareta is famous for a reason: the mussels are exceptional, fresh and full of flavor. The fish and calamari are equally great—tender, perfectly cooked, and consistently delicious. It’s the kind of place where you end up ordering more than planned simply because everything tastes so good.

Another plus is the fast, efficient service. Even when the bar is busy (and it often is), the staff keep things moving smoothly without losing their friendliness.

La Pilareta is a must-visit for anyone wanting authentic local tapas, a welcoming setting, and a taste of old Valencia done right.

[2023-04-25] This place has hands down the BEST POTATO BRAVAS in Valencia! Katarina was our server yesterday. This was a return visit and we ended up taking two servings of the Potato Bravas for a party of  four. Their aioli is great, and the tomato sauce is SO GOOD, that we ask for a serving on the side.
The drinks are also great, according to two people in our group who had them. The house drink/wine or whatever it’s called is a vermouth I think. It was so good that the person took seconds! The sangria is good, but it’s the house wine that is the star!

This bar has been open since 1917, so over a hundred years. No wonder, it’s a beautiful interior- a cross between a pub and a really lovely bar. Beautiful tikes, even in the bathrooms. The toilets, a flight of 9 steps up, including a little landing, are clean and conveniently big, unlike many popular places.

It’s the food you’ll go back for- oyster fritters, beans, a fish tapas and … all of them!

[2024-08-16] This place is just perfection. You can patiently wait to get a table or you can opt to slide on up to the bar and order your dish of steaming hot mussels. Dunk chunky pieces of bread into the broth, pierce the sweet plump mussels and eat them. Toss the shells onto the buckets on the floor. The Patatas Bravas here are uniquely served with a spicy tomato sauce and a dollop of garlicky aioli on top but honestly I didn't care about the sauce at all. The potatoes were perfect on their own. Crunchy crispy on the outside, pillowy on the inside, they had a unique texture that became an addiction. W normally don't order Patatas Bravas but Jorge our bartender told us to order it. He also recommended the cuttlefish omg. Perfectly grilled tender with bites of sea salt, it disappeared very quickly. Wash it all down with terrific white wines by the glass. Finish with the house vermouth. Divine.

[2025-03-22] Long queue, had to wait for about 1 hour to get my table which was actually promising.  I don't mind queuing for great food.

It's ok, nothing mind-blowing.   I'm sure their produce is fresh.
Somehow it was lacking overall punch in the flavors.

The sepia was a bit on the chewy side.
The mussels were actually plum and juicy just the soup was very peppery.  I guess that is their signature taste.   I really wanted to like it.   Was just not my taste.

But  for sure it was freshly  made and served quickly.

The croquettes were creamy and crunchy, golden brown yet lacking flavor. And for sure I couldn't taste the mussels.

The olives were great!

Overall it's ok.    By no means is the food bad.   Was it worth waiting for 1 hour? No.

Would I eat here for a quick bite? Yes.

The waiter was efficient and worked like a machine but I never saw him smile in the 2 hours I've been there .

[2025-10-18] I want to believe we went to the wrong bar and it wasn't the one in the Google reviews because we don't understand anything... We went 10 years ago and had a wonderful meal, but nothing like that time. They kept us waiting for an hour to be seated and another 20 minutes for someone to come to our table, and that was after 3 p.m. and there was no one in line and empty tables. The bravas were delicious, but the beans and mussels were bland as hell. Pepsi without ice, and they brought it to us with ice (we're still waiting for them to bring it back). We ordered two cuttlefish and they brought us one. We asked for still water and they brought it to us cold. When we complained about it, they told us they didn't have any still water... What??? And all around us were complaints about wrong orders. And the best part! They sat six people at a table that was no more than 50 cm in radius.

[2023-05-16] A very quirky tapas bar with a touch of the Falty towers about it. We were a group of 6 and we were expected to sit around one of their tiny round table no larger than 600 mm diameter. The service was chaotic with the only waitress shouting out the orders at the top of here voice to the only other employee which was the chef in the kitchen 20 meters away. Don't ask for sangria you will get short sharp shrift. Having said that the food was good and we managed to eat the food as quick as it arrived to keep the table free for the drinks. Considering the lack of comfort, space and welcome the cost was more than we expected. The loan waitress did get reinforcements as the evening went on who seemed to have been trained in the same school of charm as the first waitress. Go here if you want an unusual experience to chuckle in disbelief about for many years.

[2023-04-07] This is just great if you want authentic tapas in a traditional Spanish bar. Don't go if you're expecting posh service and fine dining though.
We stood at bar and were acknowledged by barmen and served after 5 minutes. The vermouth served from the barrel on bar was delicious (and strong!) and went well with the tapas.
We ordered 3 media raciones and was enough for 2; patatas bravas and calamares were great, but don't order the habas beans; brown and soft, we didn't finish them and saw several other plates of beans left by other customers.
Service was efficient and straightforward with no bells and whistles, and it's great listening to the waiters shout orders vaguely in direction of kitchen, and the food miraculously comes out as ordered 5 minutes later. A Spanish experience worth doing

[2024-10-10] La Pilareta is a charming traditional restaurant in Valencia that beautifully captures the essence of Spanish cuisine through its exceptional tapas offerings. The highlight of the menu is undoubtedly the iconic mussels, which are fresh and flavorful, setting the tone for a delightful dining experience. The grilled cuttlefish is a must-try; it's perfectly cooked, tender, and bursting with flavor. The battered squid, or calamari, is another standout, crispy on the outside while maintaining a succulent interior.

To complement the meal, La Pilareta serves a delicious traditional red vermouth, adding a perfect touch to the vibrant atmosphere. Overall, dining here is not just a meal but an immersive experience in Spanish culinary traditions that should not be missed when visiting Valencia.

[2024-03-22] 🎖️🎖️What a shame, we came back and they practically asked us to leave. It's a pity the staff isn't fully committed, as is needed right now. We asked for a table and they told us to wait. They didn't even offer us beers at the bar, so we decided to order them at the table. When we realized it, they'd already given us our table. A real shame, there were five of us and it was empty, at 3:15 pm, and there was another empty table inside, but they said that table was for eight. (Nowadays, restaurants in the new generation have communal tables.)
🎖️🎖️🎖️🎖️ Perfect spot for a quick bite of tapas or lunch before the mascletà (fireworks display). Typical dishes include clochinas (mussels), Russian salad, grilled cuttlefish—all amazing—and to top it all off, a chivito (steak sandwich).

[2026-04-10] Two adults and two children. We visited this restaurant on Wednesday, April 8, 2026, around 7 p.m. We sat outside on the terrace. I don't understand how this restaurant received such a high rating. The young waiter was sitting outside with other guests, leisurely drinking his cocktail. We had to ask him several times for a glass. He was very friendly towards us, but he listlessly threw the plates down on the table. The glass never arrived. When I briefly went inside, I saw all the dishes on the bar and the leftover food in the crates right by the entrance. Everything looked very dirty. I've visited at least 50 tapas bars in various cities in my life. Taste-wise, this one disappointed me the most. We didn't eat more than half of our food and left.

[2025-09-08] They don't have a very extensive food menu; almost everything is tapas. We arrived late for lunch. We were visiting museums in the Valencia area, and the truth is, I expected more people due to the type of establishment.

I'm particularly particular about food, since I don't like certain vegetables like peppers, onions, and others. But my friend and I both agreed that it didn't quite satisfy us.
I mean, the 80s Spanish "quids" style didn't quite remind us of the flavors and memories of that era.

At some point, we felt like we were overpowered because the waiters seemed eager to leave, and we asked before sitting down if the kitchen was open. They told us it was no problem, as it was open 24 hours.

A tapas bar, and that's it.

[2025-11-13] Since my last visit to Valencia, a few years ago, I made a point of going there specifically for their mussels with that amazing sauce that was practically so good you could mop it up with bread. But I went back two weeks ago and was sorely disappointed. Instead of the mountain of mussels they used to give me, they served me a ridiculously small portion with a sauce that was nothing like the one I was used to. It was watery, tasteless, and in short, I was completely frustrated with the meal. I don't know if they've changed owners since then, but it's nothing like it used to be, although they might have a few other decent dishes. But those mussels? I'll never be ordering them again. What a shame! I used to love this place!

[2023-10-23] Great spot for tapas near central market. We came for lunch around 1 on Monday and it was easy to get a table, but not empty. We ordered Valencian mussels, calamari, cod with peppers, patatas bravas, chorizo and an omelette. We found the omelette slight under seasoned for our taste but otherwise the food was fantastic. We especially enjoyed the mussels and the patatas were probably the best I have enjoyed in the city.
The service was friendly and we enjoyed the way that they shouted the orders to the kitchen. I wish I had tried some of the house vermouth from the barrel. Overall, some of the best tapas we have enjoyed, nice location and enjoyed the rustic quality of the place.

[2024-03-24] I love the atmosphere and decor of this place. It is such a unique restaurant with a long history so that alone makes it worthwhile to visit. The downside is that it does get packed, so you might only be standing at the bar and not have a place to sit. Food is one of the best tapas I've had in Spain (although very fish and seafood heavy). My boyfriend who doesn't eat fish or seafood had a bocadillo and he thought it was quite dry. I didn't like the service but it's also part of the vibe - as far as I understand- very distanced and factual. Don't expect smiles and friendliness. All in all pay a visit to try the mussles (entero) which is their specialty.

[2025-09-07] A somewhat unusual place, a sort of typical snack bar that a friend recommended to us for their local mussel dish. They're cooked in a stew with flavors that are a bit different from what we're used to in Italy, but we were curious; we went in the evening.
It's a somewhat unusual place, crowded at certain times, but it's worth trying a few dishes. Obviously, it's not a restaurant, so don't expect to have a proper dinner there, but we liked it anyway.
I don't know if they take reservations; we arrived and waited 10 minutes. Tables are turned over fairly quickly. Alternatively, you can grab a quick bite at the counter.

[2026-01-26] I've been to La Pilareta several times, and each visit surprises me with how well they maintain the essence of a traditional Valencian bar. The pickled mussels are spectacular, perfectly seasoned with just the right touch of acidity. The broad beans, when in season, are another must-try that I never miss. The atmosphere is authentic, lively but charming, and the bar is always bustling. The only downside is that the service can sometimes be a bit overwhelming when it's busy, but that's understandable given the small size of the place. Highly recommended for anyone wanting to try classic Valencian tapas.

[2025-08-19] This is the second time we've been, in the space of five years, and to be honest, we were a little disappointed.
We ordered mussels, patatas bravas, and esgarraet.
The mussels were good quality, but the sauce was bland.
The patatas bravas were very good.
The esgarraet didn't live up to the price; it was a small portion and very ordinary.
In short, I don't know if this is typical or if we were just off on a bad day.
Take care of your local customers and strive to ensure that your iconic establishment isn't just a tourist trap for those who come because of the
good reviews from other customers.

[2023-06-11] Honestly the only reason the star rating is 3 is because of the bad server. Food was very delicious and a must visit place in Valencia, however the server’s unexpectedly annoying attitude left a bad taste in our mouth. He would bang the drink on our table and roll his eyes if he didn’t like what we ordered. Just a bad service quality in my vacation and a bad reputation for the place and it’s people.

If you are in town during the muscles season you must go, it is the reason why this place is there. We also had other small tapas and they were quite tasty.

[2023-10-11] Let’s start with: Oh my god. So very tasty food.
Service is Spanish, don’t expect instant friendly love - it takes time to get a smile but as a Finn I respect such genuine friendliness instead of ingenuity.

This local tapas bar has been open since 1918.
Didn’t see a single tourist inside but I’m sure there are some at times.
But let’s get to the point. Food.
Amazing from first small tapas bite to bigger main dish.
Little fish, beans, Russian salad, fresh tomato salsa, pepito Valenciano, Sepia. Top taste & texture.
My personal highlight of Valencia.

[2026-01-30] We went for dinner and were quite disappointed, especially by the waiter who took our order (the taller of the two young men). He served us without looking at us, and when we asked questions about the dishes, he answered arrogantly and laughed at his colleague. It's incomprehensible that people who work with the public provide such poor service (and not just the way he treated us, but they brought our food out without plates or cutlery, and a cuttlefish, for example, shouldn't be cut or eaten with your hands...). We absolutely do not recommend it.

[2022-10-04] An iconic spot in the heart of the Carmen neighborhood, in Valencia's old town. In season, they serve the best clochinas (small mussels). They offer all kinds of tapas, including their own specialties. The building retains its original architecture. Don't be alarmed when the waiter shouts to order tapas from the kitchen—it's quite a distinctive sound. Good service, quality products at market prices. They offer tables both inside and on the terrace. Definitely a place worth visiting.
Approximate price per person: €30

[2018-07-03] We've passed by this restaurant many times, and I am so disappointed that I missed this little gem for so long.

Quite frankly, it has the tastiest mussels dishes in Valencia...simple but divine. And I have never found pescaditos fritos so delicious and plump before. The waiting staff are fast and friendly, the prices are very affordable and the wine (my favourite is their Turbio) is all really good. Seating outside if you come with a furry friend, and lots of seats inside, this is a little gem of a place.

[2025-08-19] I've been twice, once during the day and once at night.

The first time, during the day, I was happy. Prices were high, but the cuttlefish and artichokes were excellent.

The second time, at night, the esgarraet (a Catalan salad) was tasteless, with hardly any cod, and it was a half portion charged at the price of a full portion. The few mussels were nothing special. And the cuttlefish sandwich, at almost €15, is outrageous…

It's a long-established place, but these days it's very tourist-oriented.

[2026-01-04] Charming place, it could be a bit cleaner and better heated, but that's not as bad as the overall rip-off. You leave hungry, and for a half portion of tough calamari with soggy batter, and a tapa that looks about the size of a 100-piece sandwich, it costs over €7. The mussels were okay, but not completely clean and really just tasted of paprika. The broad beans were interesting, with a broth that tasted of pennyroyal, and the best thing was the fried fish. At least the service was quite friendly.

[2025-11-05] It's a small bar with few tables, but the middle-aged waiter is quite unfriendly and rude. Even if you thank him every time he brings you something, he doesn't respond or even look at you. The food is basic and unremarkable, and the location is good, close to the center. However, the staff's attitude somewhat spoils the experience; it feels like they're annoyed at serving you. A simple "thank you" or a smile would go a long way toward improving the service and making you want to return.

[2022-08-16] I went here based on a article I read and I was not disappointed! I came in mid-august and got the muscles, pescadito frito, white wine, Spanish omelette, (and lastly I read on google from another user to get the chuptios de hierbas after you eat! I was incredibly pleased by the lovely delicious food and fast service! (I went around 7:30pm because I hear it gets quite busy later on) the waiter did not speak English but was very kind and patient while I ordered in broken Spanish.

[2026-05-19] Sorry, but for me it's a no.
The staff was mediocre, the tortilla tasteless, and the mayonnaise was exorbitantly priced (we only ordered it to add some flavor). The patatas bravas (a local specialty, no less) were just okay! The potatoes were too firm, served with a simple tomato sauce (again, unseasoned) and some kind of aioli on top. The only thing that slightly redeemed the experience was the mussel croquette, which was excellent.
I do not recommend it.

[2025-07-13] A bit subpar. Got a table outside, but after drinks no one came to take the order. Went inside after half an hour and got told orders are only taken at tables outside. The patatas bravas were mediocre compared to others I’ve had here and the calamares lacked any seasoning. The other dishes we ordered were also forgetful. Even though this place is busy and in many of the must visit lists, I’d advise you to skip it if this wasn’t an off day for them.

[2019-03-30] This lovely place is recommended by most travel guides. This is rightfully so. The food is awesome, drinks lovely and the employees professional and friendly. We shared a sellection of dishes. They were all great. When asked for a recommendation our waiter suggested the cuttlefish with mayonaise. A very surprisingly excellent dish. Their house special, drink wise, is a really well done version of the negroni. Not unimportant: prices are very fair.

[2025-09-08] The only redeeming feature, and not exactly amazing, were the mussels. I've had better mussels in Galicia. The atmosphere was cold and the service was inattentive. We also ordered tapas like broad beans and patatas bravas. And they charged us for them as portions, though they weren't generous. I'm from Seville and I know what a tapa is, and what they served me was similar, but they charged me for it as a portion. I advise against visiting.

[2022-10-29] This place has always been good, but twice recently some of the staff, well the server in particular, has a real attitude . He doesn’t enjoy his  work.  A real shame. I’ve seen other comments with the same views. Great tapas and Mussels . A good experience in the heart of great bars and live music.  However we walked away before we sat down today. And just gave a 5 star rating to the cafe nearby who were  really pleased that we visited.

[2022-09-05] My wife and I came found this place last min and we're so happy we did. It ended up being our favorite food in Valencia. Very chill spot.  We went early thankfully because it got packed quick and were able to sit outside. Everything we tried was good but we loved the croquettes, calimari and mussels most. Our big surprise though was the grilled cuttlefish! We'd definitely recommend trying that If you love seafood, particularly squid.

[2019-05-09] Went here because it had good reviews. Wasn't happy. These dishes are not tapas. I would expect to get small basic pieces like these free with a drink, like you do everywhere else in Spain, the more they care - the nicer and better quality the freebies are. I paid for these, expecting proper dishes. Not good.  Wouldn't recommend.. and why is it more to sit in the street than on the premises,  and a smile wouldn't hurt either

[2024-10-21] I got a free table outside. Went inside to grab the menu and sat down again when the man from behind the bar came outside and told me this table was for 2 ladies who entered after me. Then the other waiter came back and they had a kind of argue about it. I could stay but left anyway. The face of the bartender was annoying. There are more tapas restaurants. Because they have a name its NOT necessary to be THIS arrogant.

[2022-09-06] We came with a party of 14 and had a wonderful time!  The tapas were excellent.  Everything we shared was delicious!

I don't love mussels in the United States because they prepare them poorly, this restaurant prepared them perfectly - they were absolutely delicious.  The jamón ibérico was some of the best I've had, ever!  The bread was fresh, the beer cold, and overall excellent experience.

Hope to go back someday!

[2023-12-26] One of my favorite places to eat some tapas with cañas in the heart of El Carmen. It has been a classic, well known for decades for its "clotxinas", the valencian local mussels. Also very popular are some other tapas such as the grilled squid, bravas potatoes, and "Pepitos", a fried sandwich, very tasty. Get their tap beer (delicious and refreshing), a perfect companion to enjoy their tapa selection

[2026-01-02] There were two of us, and we were still drinking soft drinks, not wine or vermouth. We found the price high for the small portions. I recommend the patatas bravas, although we ordered two portions because the first one wasn't enough; in fact, I thought they were half portions. The fried fish wasn't crispy at all, and the mussels had a chorizo ​​aftertaste (I suppose that's how they make it).

[2026-01-13] We returned after 25 years and the place hasn't changed; it's as if time has stood still.
The waiters were attentive and quick.
The beers are expensive, I think we all agree on that. And the mussels (clochinas, as far as we know, are only available between April and August in Valencia) were bland and overcooked. Anyway, it's always worth stopping by for a beer and trying their other tapas.

[2020-01-27] Historical value to visit, as decor typical of decades ago, yet well kept up
Tapas are good, especially I'd recommend the calamar, as they use different batter.
The typical dish here is mussels, but portion was quite small in my opinion for cost.
Service is not fast, but amusing to see how they call in the orders and announce them when they arrive, again typical of decades ago, so quaint

[2026-04-23] A characteristic Valencian restaurant where you can enjoy quality seafood, with excellent dishes served simply and without too many frills. The staff is very attentive and caring, making guests feel welcomed with great courtesy. The restaurant is simple, but precisely because of this, it exudes an authentic and familiar atmosphere, almost like being at home. The food is truly excellent.

[2024-02-16] Started off with an encounter with a grumpy middle-aged waiter, bordering on rude. We were then served by a very tired looking but gracious young lady. The whole experience was very immersive and authentic! We really enjoyed the hustle and bustle, rustic tapas, shouting orders to the kitchen, and lots  of natives eating there, the best testament of how good a good Spanish tapas bar is!

[2025-08-27] A typical place. They don't serve you an aperitif like we're used to in Madrid; if you order olives, they charge you for them. The typical "clochinas" are local mussels, very delicious, I recommend them. I don't recommend ordering esparreget here; they're canned peppers. The vermouth is typical here, but I've had better ones. A vermouth like this goes to your head right away, haha.

[2026-04-06] Came for mussels, was told that they are not available and was suggested to order artichokes which I usually love. They were dry and tasteless what I addressed twice to staff - both times they completely ignored me, changed nothing and I was left with this horrible miserable dish even there were almost no other tables to tend. Very poor service and attitude, not recommended

[2025-08-23] Very bad this time in August. We went on Friday for mussels, Russian salad, and two sandwiches: one calamari and one pork loin with cheese. We're regular customers and were completely disappointed with the service. The sandwiches never arrived, and the waiters' attitude, despite the place being half empty, was dismissive and unprofessional. We were furious and disappointed.

[2025-12-06] Came twice in two days.

Night one really good service and excellent food.
Night two the service anything but acceptable. Tables didnt get cleaned, service was extremely slow,
and unfriendly. Waiters where making out during service.

To the owner: While we were there several people sat at tables but left because they were un-attended.

Overall good experience

[2022-05-21] This place should be tried at least once. The owner is so friendly and cheerful. Also the prices are cheap! The foods are amazing especially Pepita and Esgarra. By the way a whole squid (sepia) is too much for a person. You should demand a  shot of Chupito de hierbas after the meal. This shot is a traditional liquor by made of mixed herbs which must be tried.

[2026-05-16] As a Valencian native and a big fan of food, I'm sorry to say I don't understand why people order clochinas. It's a dish full of salty broth that really ruins the flavor of the mussels, which were cooked perfectly. The other tapas we ordered were good, but I was truly surprised that the clochinas weren't up to par. The homemade vermouth was very good.

[2026-04-10] I want to highlight the excellent service of Jordi, the waiter at La Pilareta bar. His customer service is impeccable: always efficient, polite, and highly professional. It's clear he has a great sense of etiquette and pays attention to every detail, making the experience even more enjoyable. Without a doubt, he's a great asset to the establishment.

[2024-06-04] 5 stars in any department may seem over the top, here is my explanation for this rating: I love Spain and claim to know it very well despite of its size. Valencia is definitely not in my good books. Take overtourism as one of the reasons. This bar is as authentic as it gets in the old city. Try the Vermouth. The Croquetas are, in Spanish, bestial.

[2026-01-08] Our favorite place in Valencia! The food is impeccable as always, the cuisine is fantastic (top-quality ingredients), and while the waiters change regularly, Jaime is a delight—polite, attentive, and friendly. We go every week, so we consider ourselves regulars…and I have nothing but praise for him. I highly recommend the croquettes! Best regards.

[2025-10-18] Bar in the "old style". It retains the air of years ago....and a bottle from back then!!
Only one person serving the tables inside and the extension outside!!....
The kitchen keeps bringing out dishes.
Specialty: steamed clóchines (mussels) (pst!!)
Better, other dishes and breaded dishes.
Health and Peace!
...and may we win the Christmas lottery!!

[2025-10-01] We went here on the recommendation of our hosts, and it was really great. The food was typically Spanish, very tasty, and freshly prepared. Little English is spoken, but pointing and two numbers in Spanish are enough. 😉 The atmosphere is simple and authentic, but not particularly cozy (inside). Be sure to try the vermouth from the barrel.

[2025-07-16] Cool retro atmosphere in the heart of the old town. Probably the best home made Vermouth in Valencia and the tapas are fabulous. You just cant find tastier pescados fritos than here! Super friendly and professional staff with a real understanding of quality service. A totally " must do " stop off for an authentic Valencian experience.

[2026-04-21] I was welcomed and seated, but then completely ignored. After 20 minutes of waiting with no one coming to my table, the guy who had seated me left (probably end of shift). He saw me looking at him but continued on his way without saying anything. It's a shame they didn't pay much attention to me; I would have loved to try their menu.

[2025-09-05] We had lunch here. It was quiet, and we ordered a few tapas, which were clearly freshly prepared in the kitchen. You can see into the kitchen and saw two ladies hard at work, which immediately gives a good, artisanal feel.

The food was delicious, and the portions were generous. Definitely a nice place to grab a bite to eat or drink.

[2025-09-17] I've never had a potato omelet like this in my life. The potatoes were cooked and hard, the egg was practically nonexistent, and it was bland.
The place was old, the tables were dirty, the restrooms were best avoided unless absolutely necessary, and the waiters were unprofessional. It was a complete disaster from beginning to end.

[2026-03-24] It's worth queuing and waiting. La Pilareta never disappoints; we come every year, we were there last Friday and it's still the same—great quality, everything is delicious. I recommend: the clochines (mussels), Russian salad, the toast with black pudding, fried anchovies... everything is excellent and the service is always great!

[2025-10-25] This place is famous for its clotxinas and mussels... but when we were there, it wasn't clotxinas season, and they served us mussels with an unbearable broth. It tasted awful.
I don't know if it's changed owners... but other times I've been in Valencia, it was packed... but when we were there, there was only one other table.

[2024-05-28] This is one of the little charming tapas bar in central Valencia with a long history. We love the small plates we had & the vermouths we consumed. Also love the way they keep the traditions up by having the food waste troughs there. The cuttlefish fish was insanely good, so was the huge croquette looking Pepito Valenciano.

[2021-09-08] A humiliating and unpleasant experience. We were at Fallas from 12 noon until closing time. There were about 10 of us, and we really went all out, drinking tons of beers, shots, and cocktails, eating, and spending almost €100 per person. It's incredibly expensive, and the service was rude and unfriendly. A friend asked for toilet paper because they were out, and they refused to give it to her, asking why she wanted it. They didn't even offer us a complimentary shot, wouldn't let us use the restroom, told us we were unpleasant, hurled insults and impertinent remarks at us, called us annoying—they were truly unpleasant people (in the process of adopting inclusive language). They called us annoying personally and refused to give us a complaint form. A complete disaster. We will never return. We recommend Cómic Café, where the staff are friendly and fair. We were all shocked by the terrible treatment we received.

We didn't leave earlier because there was no room anywhere else; it was Fallas, and everything was booked. Had we known that, we obviously wouldn't have gone there. And of course, we spent a lot of money. We were polite to them the whole time until they started treating us badly, and we responded to their rudeness and commented on their sour faces. When we asked for toilet paper, it was because the restrooms were open; otherwise, obviously, no one would have been inside, and that person wouldn't have asked for toilet paper. One of the employees' responses was, "What do you want toilet paper for?" among many other gems. We understand they were overwhelmed with work, but they treated us badly, period. It wasn't our fault. An apology would have been enough. When we arrived in the morning, we were very friendly and understanding, and there was no problem with the first waitress. It was in the afternoon, when the shift changed, that they started speaking rudely to us, and it seemed like our presence bothered them. We weren't the rude ones; otherwise, we wouldn't have been offended. In fact, many of my friends have been or are waiters and we empathize a lot, always trying to make their day easier.

[2023-04-05] For what they offer, I think it's expensive. The double portions also seem expensive for the place it is, and well, the service from some of the waitresses wasn't great. But hey, we have plenty of other options. Personally, I didn't like anything they offered or what I saw, and honestly, I think this place is for tourists, not locals. Regarding the complimentary snack, yes, many places do it, and it's something many people appreciate. If you haven't noticed, stop with the oil and other spices and just offer regular cocoa. If others can have it, so can you, because that little touch is really appreciated. Mercadona and Makro sell bags of it, and without all the fuss about olive oil and other nonsense. 👌

Reply to the owner: You don't take reviews well. I have every right to express my opinion, and if you don't like it, don't waste your time. I spend it as I see fit. Bored? No, I'm not bored; that's what Google reviews are for.

Regarding the fact that I read the comments, it's to assess what they say and agree or disagree, but not to "mess with you." Reflect on it and try to improve, not confront, because if you do this, it doesn't reflect well on your management skills.

[2025-03-16] The service at this bar is like when you go out to dinner with your friends and your ex is in the group; it's awkward, forced, cold, and you want to leave as soon as possible. We were treated in a way that could have been the subject of the Nuremberg trials. The guy at the door kept repeating the phrase "the bathroom is for guests only" to a poor girl who was half-fainted from the combination of heat, crowds, and her period, which made everyone there see a lack of humanity worthy of the famous phrase "we were just following orders." On the other hand, the girl who served us on the terrace brought us drinks already prepared at the bar, without being able to tell which terrace they had set up, and holding them with her fingers inside the bar. The worst part was that when we expressed our displeasure, she insulted us and told us that since we were in fallas, we should be grateful for being served. Finally, the bathrooms had a level of hygiene worthy of an abandoned zoo, as there were also live and dead animals.

[2024-02-17] During the Carnival parade, we were all sitting down to order something, and only we were told we had to vacate our table so other people could sit down, even though all the other tables were also occupied and our drinks were just filled with ice. We could have left peacefully, like in any other bar, without having to order more drinks.

To answer your comment: Nobody sat for three hours just for fun. If you recall, there was someone who joined us later, and when I told them there would be four of us in total, they put out four coasters. Also, there was a couple next to us who had been there before us, also without ordering anything else to stay. Theoretically, you should have asked people to leave in order, first them, since they had been there longer and hadn't ordered anything, and then us. That's how it works in your establishment.

Besides, you only need to look at the feedback to see that it wasn't just us. :)

[2022-03-19] I didn't know this place, which is quite cozy... I was there this week for the Fallas festival and I loved the friendly and welcoming service from the waitresses so much that it's become my favorite spot for beers and tapas. The tapas were spectacular, the croquettes so varied and homemade, each one more delicious than the last. The chicken tenders were amazing, the baby squid tender, and the Mery sauce with chopped almonds was a really nice touch. Everything was fresh and homemade. In general, all the tapas were very generous and delicious. We also tried the sandwiches... Mmm, good quality meat and well-filled. We really loved them. And what can I say about the desserts? The homemade cakes were delicious. Congratulations to the cook and her team. We will definitely continue to frequent this place. I recommend it 100%.

[2022-10-15] LAMENTABLE service. After spending almost €200 and paying the bill, the waiter approached us to tell us that €1.60 was missing, which turned out to be for some peanuts they'd given us (which they told us they were giving us because we had almost €200 on our bill). We proceeded to pay in cash and ended up being short 30 cents, mistaking a 50-cent coin for a 20-cent one. The waiter then came back to rudely tell us (I quote): "I told you €1.60, not €1.40, which is what you gave me. I made it very clear."
You must be really mean and greedy to want to charge us for peanuts after spending €186, which they didn't offer for free, considering all the food we were eating. What a lack of professionalism and customer service. Disgraceful.

[2025-03-23] As the name suggests, it seems the owner, manager, or whoever is taking payments at the bar isn't very sharp, despite the name of the place. After having a few drinks and going to pay, she didn't take kindly to us paying for five drinks one by one. She verbally told us it was a hassle. Her unpleasant attitude and behavior continued when she refused to accept a card payment for a drink (something that's accepted everywhere). She told my friend that it was "childish," creating a very unpleasant situation. You can't treat a friendly customer in such a rude and arrogant way. On top of that, it's more expensive than any other place in the area. I'm never going back. Not recommended at all.

[2019-09-14] Pathetic service, we left without dinner.
Two of us arrived today, Saturday, at 9:35 PM. Our drinks were served immediately. We ordered salad, chicken legs, a sandwich, and a mini-burger.
At 11:15 PM, the salad arrived, while the chicken legs were quickly served to other tables that had arrived much later. At 11:40 PM, we told the waitress, and she apologized profusely, saying that the chicken legs would be brought to us shortly.
A while later, the sandwich and wings were brought to the table next to us, which had arrived 40 minutes later.
At 11:55 PM, we asked for the bill and left.
PATHETIC, to say the least, after an hour and a half with hardly any food. We won't be back.

[2022-04-16] Very, very unrecommendable place. I had a few beers and they didn't even offer hot chocolate, you had to pay the price of 1.60, unbelievable. But the most unfortunate thing is that when my companion took the last sip and left it on the table, they took it away from him in less than a second. But the best part is now when I finished mine while still holding it in my hand, they snatched it out of my hand. How rude and unwilling to throw you out of the place after barely 20 minutes. Zero customer service and no kindness at all, because if they had taken it away and asked me if I wanted another one, they would have been much better. But taking it out of my hand...

[2025-03-23] I've been a couple of times and haven't liked the way customers are treated. Even so, since it's in the Ruzafa neighborhood and the terraces are always packed, I keep going because their terrace is cozy and it's not easy to find a spot.
The worst part is that today I witnessed the bar owner arguing with some customers. Besides being disrespectful and hurling insults, I think it's UNACCEPTABLE!!!

They treat customers badly, the food isn't anything to write home about, and they charge a fortune for what is, to put it mildly, passable. But the worst part is how incompetent they are with their customers.

I'm giving it one star because I have to.

[2023-05-05] Pretty bad… of everything we ordered, the worst was the Agua de Valencia, which was exactly that, WATER. A ton of ice. And the pork loin toast was incredibly dry, without even a single tomato on the bread; we only left half of it. All the tapas we ordered were around €9 or more (all mediocre), more than in many places in Barcelona (laughable!). We're from there, and we thought: how is that possible? And to top it all off, since I have friends who own restaurants, I politely told the waitress everything, as constructive criticism, and NOTHING! They charged us anyway, as if nothing had happened. Very poor business management. Disgraceful.

[2025-03-23] BEWARE! Everything seems fine: the terrace, the location, and the friendly waitress. Until it's time to ask for the bill.
Besides being faced with an exorbitant price for the terrible quality they offer, you meet the owner (or manager): an unfriendly and very unpleasant woman, with absolutely no idea how to treat customers. Just seeing the poor employees who have to put up with that every day, and whose faces made it clear to us immediately, I not only WILL NOT RETURN, but I ABSOLUTELY DO NOT RECOMMEND IT.

PS: I came to this page to see if it was just my perception. But I see now that it's a common occurrence at this establishment.

[2026-03-13] Yesterday, I went with two Spanish friends. I'm a foreigner visiting, and I love filming all the pretty things. I asked if I could film because I loved the place. The staff smiled at me, and I assumed I could. Then a tall, blonde girl with a ponytail, wearing a nose ring, came up to me and stood face-to-face in a loud voice, scolding me for filming the place and especially her coworkers. I explained that it was just the general atmosphere, but she still got upset, and I felt very uncomfortable. I had a bad time. We ordered tapas, beer, and a glass of wine—everything was delicious, but the girl was awful. I felt uncomfortable and 😢

[2024-03-30] The worst place I've ever eaten calamari, well, calamari, if you can even call it that, and on top of that, €9 for a tiny portion... And then they have the nerve to call it "Andalusian-style Calamari"? It's an insult to Andalusian cuisine. Go there and you'll see what REAL calamari and a proper portion are... Like I said... Typical tourist trap 👎🏼👎🏼👎🏼... The service is good and fast, though... RESPONSE: SERIOUSLY, IF YOU THINK THAT SHAMEFUL PORTION IS A PORTION OF ANDALUSIAN-STYLE CALAMARI... I INVITE YOU TO GO TO MALAGA OR ALMERIA AND ORDER IT... YOU'LL SEE WHAT I'M TALKING ABOUT... SERIOUSLY... LEARN... 😘

[2020-12-08] It's not bad for having a drink on the terrace. They clean the tables, and the waitress was friendly and quick to serve us. They bring a small glass jar of complimentary corn nuts and peanuts to go with the drinks. It's common in Valencia for them not to offer anything, except for lunches where they seem to be doing you a favor by giving you peanuts and olives... They have COVID-19 safety measures in place, and you can pay by card. We didn't eat there, but the food at the next table looked good. Looking at the menu, the prices aren't cheap, so we'll have to try it another time. It's quite busy.

[2026-02-06] It's a small but comfortable place. Four of us visited on Saturday, January 31, 2026, for tapas and dinner, and we left very happy.
We ordered Padrón peppers, Russian salad, cured ham, broken eggs with ham, caramelized cheese, four assorted croquettes, a slice of Iberian ham toast, two double 1906 beers, two Alhambra 1925 beers, a non-alcoholic beer, two soft drinks, a bottle of water, and two Aperols. Everything was beautifully presented, well-served, quick, and delicious.
We paid €102.20, a little over €25.50 per person. The quality/price ratio is good. We will definitely return.

[2025-11-24] We were lucky and there was a table for two, and we had a very good dinner.
The atmosphere is pleasant, although it was a bit chilly because the door was open.
The service was very quick and friendly.
The food was excellent.
The Russian salad was delicious, and the black pudding scramble was spectacular and a generous portion.
The toasts were a good size, although the bread was, in my opinion, a bit too crispy (or toasted), but they were very good. We tried the hummus and the salmon toasts.
Good tinto de verano (red wine and lemonade).
We'll be back. I recommend it 100%.

[2025-03-24] The Malvaloca Tavern in Ruzafa, Valencia, is a true culinary treasure. With its cozy atmosphere and thoughtful decor, it offers a gastronomic experience that never disappoints. The dishes are a perfect combination of tradition and creativity, with fresh, quality ingredients that shine in every bite. The service is exceptional, with attentive staff always willing to recommend the best pairing or explain the details of each recipe. Without a doubt, the Malvaloca Tavern has earned a place in Ruzafa, ideal for both a quiet meal and a special evening. Highly recommended!

[2022-12-03] A very pleasant place, in the heart of Ruzafa. The terrace is pleasant, and they offer blankets to keep you warm.
The dishes were excellent. We ordered Russian salad, spicy chorizo, and, without a doubt, the best, the croquettes (with black pudding and boletus mushrooms). They were homemade and spectacular. Reasonable prices. The service was brilliant. Carmen waited on us the whole time, making sure we were comfortable and getting us served as quickly as possible. It's a pleasure to go to places where you're treated so well! We'll definitely be back!

[2025-03-24] The Malvaloca Tavern in Ruzafa is a charming place that stands out not only for its excellent food but also for its cozy and vibrant atmosphere. The fusion of traditional flavors with innovative touches in their dishes is simply stunning. Every bite is an explosion of freshness and flavor, making every visit a memorable experience. The staff is exceptionally friendly, always willing to recommend the best items on the menu. A perfect place to enjoy a relaxed meal or to celebrate a special occasion. Without a doubt, one of the best options in Valencia!

[2025-12-15] Hi! On Saturday, December 13th, we had a reservation at another place in the area, but due to technical problems, they cancelled our table at the last minute. Desperate, I started calling several restaurants in Ruzafa, but no one could make room for us... then I remembered this place, Malvaloca, and thought, I'll give it a try! And what luck! Carmen answered the phone, she was lovely! She made room for 15 of us just like that, from one day to the next, and she was so kind! We had a fantastic meal and felt right at home. Thank you, Carmen!

[2018-06-07] The food is delicious, but the portions are far too small for the price. Also, the "patatas bravas" aren't really "bravas" at all; they're just large, roasted potatoes sliced ​​lengthwise. The place itself is nice, but the service is terrible. I went on a Saturday, and by 4 pm they were already clearing everything away, giving us dirty looks and gesturing for us to leave. Furthermore, when they were clearing some of our plates, they took the sauces without asking.

In short: tasty food, but small portions, expensive, and bad service.

[2023-10-30] Excellent food, pleasant atmosphere, and good prices.
The waitresses are very friendly and attentive to their customers.
They have a daily menu on Sundays that varies. This is what we tried. They offered us three starters, a choice of main course, bread and dessert, or coffee. It's hearty and satisfying.
They serve both inside and outside the restaurant.
The decor is inviting.
The patatas bravas, as well as the side dishes, are original and delicious. The carrot cake is homemade and very good. The rice pudding is very filling.

[2024-10-27] It was a terrible experience. The prices are exorbitant for what they offer. I understand that rents are expensive in the area, but at least if I order a "toast" sandwich, it should be edible. Furthermore, you order a "double" beer and it's not even the size of a small draft beer. It's quite pathetic...

I'll be amused to see how the owner takes this review badly, just like he has with the others, responding arrogantly instead of using a negative review as an opportunity to improve and correct the mistakes they're making.

[2025-03-23] One of the worst experiences I've ever had eating out.
Extremely high prices for the poor quality they offer, and the owner's lack of manners, which, judging by previous reviews, I don't think is the first time.

RESPONSE: You don't know how to behave or even read. I don't know where I wrote about eating? I think that just demonstrates your poor management once again, given the numerous negative reviews you have. You should get yourself checked out. There are professionals for what you do.

[2022-10-02] It had been a while since I'd been here! This morning I sat down for a beer. They brought it to me, and after a while, seeing that they weren't bringing anything to go with it (they used to give a small bowl of nuts), I asked if they could give me something. They said yes, but I'd have to pay extra. €1.60 for some peanuts! I understand things are tough, but you can get peanuts with your drink anywhere in Valencia... We were going to meet for lunch, and now we've lost interest. Details...

[2025-03-24] It's a gem that combines the best of traditional cuisine with a modern and creative touch. The atmosphere is very pleasant, ideal for enjoying a meal in good company. Every dish is made with quality ingredients, and it shows in the flavor, from the simplest tapas to the most elaborate options. The staff is friendly and professional, always smiling and ready to make you feel at home. Without a doubt, a must-visit for food lovers in the heart of Ruzafa. An experience worth repeating!

[2020-09-04] Hi guys,

This is Pablo again, now in Valencia.

We have taken dinner in La Malvaloca tonight. It is one of the best places I've ever been in the Mediterranean coast of Spain. The tapas and the sandwiches were simply amazing and you should try 'Agua de Valencia' here. However, I have to mention the good attitude of the waiters, they created a really good atmosphere in our dinner, I am really thankful to them. We will come back for sure.

Best regards,

Pablo.

[2026-01-19] Everything was excellent. We went for lunch as a group of 16. They made the reservation without any problem, and Carmen and her team were wonderful. They put up with us and handled the noise from so many people 😂. The food was a selection of tapas: patatas bravas, calamari, chicken tenders—all very good and freshly made. It's truly a top spot for drinks or tapas. They are wonderful people, friendly and very kind. Thank you so much, Malvaloca team! ❤️

[2021-12-20] Great place for tapas in Ruzafa. I'd always gone there to get drunk, but not for a proper meal. Everything was really, really good. The salads are fantastic, and the best thing, without a doubt, is the "patatas a lo pobre" (poor man's potatoes). They're like thick chips with egg and chistorra sausage/ham. A dream! The service is very good, they're always very friendly, although the food could come out a tad faster, that's my only suggestion.

[2023-05-05] A truly awful experience, and I absolutely do not recommend it.

We ordered a toasted sandwich with pork loin, which was incredibly dry, with no oil, no sauce, and I couldn't even eat it. Furthermore, the pitcher of Valencian water was 80% ice and practically no drink.

I'm from Barcelona, ​​used to high prices, but here I paid even more for the terrible quality. I'm sure a cheaper restaurant like 100 Montaditos would be much better.

[2024-04-12] A group of six of us came from Tardeo, and the waiters were incredibly friendly and attentive to our needs. We ended up having dinner here, and it didn't disappoint. They have a very varied menu, and everything was delicious. We'll definitely be back; we felt very comfortable, and the atmosphere in the area is fantastic. A special thanks to our waitress, Carmen, for her excellent service. We'll definitely be back soon.

[2024-03-17] I've been there a couple of times during Fallas festival, and the truth is that it's a place with a great vibe, with very attentive and helpful waitresses. I was there yesterday with four friends, and they gave us a table right away. Then four more friends came, and despite the crowd, they didn't cause any problems (we were short on chairs, and the waitress went out of her way to get us more). It's always a good idea!

[2020-11-14] My friends and I came for dinner and we loved it. The appetizers were beautifully presented and delicious, and I especially loved the vegan burger, recommended by Carmen. The waitress, along with her colleague, provided outstanding service. I highly recommend it, and especially the team who treat you so well and make you feel right at home, which is so appreciated during these times of Covid. Thank you so much!

[2022-10-12] A lovely little terrace in the heart of Russafa. Very friendly service. The best part. Now... I understand that everything is incredibly expensive, but charging 9 euros for 5 potato wedges, half of them raw... everything else was great. The scrambled eggs with black pudding and spring garlic were sublime. We'll be back. I insist, the best thing was the service. Everyone was very friendly.

[2025-12-26] The atmosphere was good due to the number of bars around, and the croquettes were good. Unfortunately, on two occasions I saw one of the waiters asking the waitress who was serving us to come to our table because we were calling her, and she shrugged because she was smoking her cigarette and then went to do something else. We didn't feel it was worth ordering another beer and we left.

[2025-03-23] A place I'll never return to. The terrace is nice, but the prices are exorbitant, the tapas are terrible, and the owner, who charges at the bar, is unpleasant, rude, and has no manners. She should do something else because she doesn't know how to run a bar. Also, from what I see in the reviews, it's a recurring thing, and people complain about the owner's insanity and lack of manners.

[2022-12-12] La Malvaloca’s modern take on classic tapas plates like huevos rotos, patatas bravas, foie, and their delicious gourmet croquetas was a breath of fresh air. The place was clean and colourful and they even had the TV off during a Spanish World Cup match - my kinda place! The decor was a little iffy in some respects, but the great food and friendly staff certainly made up for that

[2023-01-31] We were already familiar with La Malvaloca from previous visits, and I still think they're very professional. Even during Fallas (a major Valencian festival), they treated us very well. I like many things about their dishes, but especially the croquettes, which you can tell are homemade, and the different, very personal flavors. We'll definitely be back. I highly recommend them.

[2026-04-25] When I went to pay, he told me he wouldn't accept less than 10 euros. Although it was easy enough to get to 10, since a glass of wine costs 4.50. One of the most expensive places in Valencia. If only the service had been good or decent, but it wasn't even that; he was one of the rudest people I've ever met. I don't usually leave reviews, but this time I'm never going back.

[2018-08-23] Last night my partner and I decided to have dinner here. There were three of us, including my daughter. At first, I wasn't impressed after reading the menu, but once our order arrived, I liked it more and more. The quality of the dishes was quite good, as was their presentation. I'm looking forward to going back and trying other things. 100% recommended.

[2023-06-02] We ordered a tapa of "Cabrales" (Cabrales cheese spread on stale bread) and two double beers, and they charged us almost €20 (€18.20 to be exact). The Cabrales cheese was like a block of clay/Play-Doh, with five pieces of toast that looked like they'd been there forever. The service was fine, but the prices were outrageous... WE WILL NOT BE BACK.

[2017-12-11] Very good tapas bar in the heart of Russafa, which is synonymous with a good atmosphere always. Of the tapas, don't leave without trying the bravas, which are delicious! The only downside: they have few sandwich options for vegetarians or vegans

It tends to be quite busy on weekends, but it's not too difficult to find a place if you wait a bit.

[2020-07-18] A highly sought-after terrace, spacious and well-located. The service and waitress service were excellent, especially from Micaela, who was extremely attentive and attentive to the customer. The prices aren't cheap, and it's advisable to check the bill and make sure the amounts charged correspond to the menu price. Anyone can make a mistake.

[2017-04-16] Honestly, I don't understand the bad reviews. Are you sure you've eaten here?
The food is fantastic, both for lunch and dinner.
The Russian salad, the hummus, the cod croquettes, the sandwiches, and much more... the beer is nice and cold, and there's a good selection. The desserts are amazing... keep it up, girls!
You guys are awesome!

[2022-04-01] Poor service. The girl with glasses was very unpleasant. We were told that olives were only given to the owner's daughter after she and six people had spent over €50 on drinks and food. The manager was very polite and wanted to resolve the issue, but it was a bad experience. Thank you to the manager for trying to mediate.

[2023-05-06] We went for dinner and everything was delicious! We ordered patatas bravas and chicken tenders, and they were fantastic!! The desserts were outstanding; we ordered cheesecake, and it was the best I've ever had! The service was wonderful; they were attentive to us at all times. I highly recommend it—a top-notch tavern.

[2024-04-11] We arrived at the place and tried to sit down because they had many empty tables, but they claimed they were fully booked. There were no reservations. They simply refused to accept us from the group. Deplorable.

They even responded rudely. Just read the rest of the comments. I absolutely do not recommend this place.

[2022-10-24] The three-star rating is due to the somewhat exorbitant prices for certain dishes: you can't charge a whopping 9 euros for a plate of six strips of breaded chicken, or 7 for a plate of patatas bravas. Even if they do. The waitresses were also a bit confused, as they brought one of our dishes to the table next to us.

[2024-11-10] Very good cheese and spinach croquettes as well as salmon and cream cheese toasties. The waiters were friendly and helpful. The toilet was clean as well. The menu is nothing special, your regular tapas menu but the food  we ordered was very good. Indoors and outdoors seating in a lively neighbourhood. Worth trying.

[2024-12-06] We loved it! The service was fast, super friendly, and professional. The homemade food was delicious, and the price was fantastic. The festive menu at €15.95 was a steal for how tasty and generous the portions were. ❤️ It has a lovely terrace if it's sunny, and the interior is nice too. We'll definitely be back!

[2024-04-12] I came with a friend; we were coming from a concert and didn't know where to have dinner, and we absolutely loved it. It's in Ruzafa, a great place to stay afterward and have a drink. The staff are very friendly, and the tattooed waitress gave us special attention; she was very pleasant and kind. We'll be back.

[2023-01-31] We went to dinner with some friends, and everything was spectacular. The homemade croquettes are highly recommended, and the spicy bravas are also delicious! Everything was delicious, and the service was excellent. Carmen served us, she was lovely. Overall, everything was great! We'll definitely be back!

[2024-02-04] Restaurant with a wide selection of excellent, expertly prepared tapas. A great atmosphere both inside and out, and always well-served by attentive and professional staff. Special mention to Carmen for her attentiveness and friendliness. The patatas bravas are a 10/10. One of the best taverns in Ruzafa.

[2022-05-28] One of Ruzafa's most iconic taverns, where you can savor an exquisite oxtail stew and a delicious potato salad. Don't forget to order Elena's homemade croquettes, and to top it all off, the cakes.
None of this gastronomic experience would be so satisfying without the friendly and excellent service.

[2020-01-03] Overall, it was fine... the tapas were good (nothing out of the ordinary). I recommend the scrambled eggs with black pudding, but DO NOT EVER CHOOSE the mushroom carpaccio... it's the most bland, expensive, and dry thing on the menu. The service was very average for a typical bar/restaurant...

[2017-07-01] The place has a pretty good variety of dishes and snacks.

The waiters are very polite and provide excellent service.

The food is delicious, but the prices are a bit high, especially for some of the dishes. Even so, I've been there for dinner several times and will continue to visit.

[2022-05-23] We ordered several tapas and I enjoyed this meal at the start; however one of our dishes was forgotten by the waitress - we had to wave for attention to ask for it after we were in the middle of our other tapas. A couple hours after leaving this restaurant, I was unfortunately sick.

[2022-05-03] Fantastic bar in the heart of Ruzafa for enjoying beers and tapas with friends. The patatas bravas are amazing, and the homemade desserts are to die for—I highly recommend the cheesecake and the chocolate cake 🤤. The waitresses who served us were very friendly. We'll be back!

[2020-09-09] I came to Valencia for vacations and I must say that being a vegan in Valencia isn’t easy! But La Malvaloca had a Vegan Burger that wasn’t in the menu that is mind blowing! I HAD TO ORDER TWO! The best vegan burger I had in Valencia so far! The service was wonderful as well!

place: Maybean Cafe | Brunch | Lunch | Specialty Coffee
reviewTexts:
[2025-10-06] Incredible discovery. I was looking for something healthy and delicious to eat, I FOUND IT!
We ordered the egg toast (they no longer make poached eggs after 1:00 PM, and they don't have mayo anymore). They adapted it without any problem. We added the same egg and a delicious sauce. We also ordered a bowl, although after the amazing taste of the toast, it seemed more typical, but it was still delicious. We ordered kombucha and several desserts. I highly recommend the peanut butter tart; we also tried the energy bowl and the roadside cake, and I'll stick with the former.
Plus, specialty coffee and many tea combinations, including matcha tea... I definitely hope to return!!! 💚
Of course, we stayed outside because it felt quite chilly inside, which would be the only thing I liked least.

[2020-10-30] A very cozy place with excellent service, attention to detail, and healthy food with high-quality ingredients. We ordered a salmon toast to share, a tofu bowl, and a chicken bowl. For dessert, we had a pistachio protein ball and vegan cheesecake (we'd never tried it before and were amazed), all spectacular and at a very reasonable price. If you like this type of food, this place is highly recommended. We will definitely return.

[2026-04-18] For brunch and healthy meals, everything is absolutely delicious. I highly recommend ordering the chicken bowl if you're having lunch. The coffee is fantastic, and all the staff are incredibly attentive and friendly. A 10 out of 10! Inside, they have a lovely sunny terrace because it's on a pedestrian street. If you go, try the peanut tart—it's amazing!

[2022-06-26] I really enjoyed it, even though I ordered this bowl because it was the only one without avocado, and they included it anyway; I'm intolerant to avocado. Otherwise, it was delicious. I hope they add a dish with pico de gallo as a topping, as it's very good. Pictured: Hummus Bowl €9.90

[2022-12-29] If you're looking for healthy and delicious food, "MayBean" is the place for you. With countless toasts, vegan options, and smoothies, this spot offers everything from a full brunch to homemade desserts. Don't miss the roasted sweet potato with peanut sauce and the salmon bowl.

[2023-04-18] The food was delicious, especially the Chicken Bowl. They have many healthy options at very affordable prices! For dessert, we tried the cheesecake, which was absolutely spectacular, and the red velvet cake made with beets, which was also very tasty but had a stronger flavor!

[2022-07-06] My favorite café! I love their vegan brunch. The staff is always very friendly, and the terrace is perfect for bringing the dogs (who, by the way, are always greeted with a bowl of fresh water). Even though it's a bit far for us, we go back regularly! I highly recommend it.

[2022-09-24] It's located a bit off the beaten track, but the vegan options for brunch, lunch or dessert are really nice. Tried the tofu bowl and the carrot cake, which were both delicious. The prices are also reasonable, only the portion size of the bowls could be bigger.

[2026-03-24] Cute small cafe with lots of vegan options. I absolutely loved their Tofu bowl and pink latte which had nice hints of cinnamon and cardamom. I also tried vegan carrot cake with cashew & lime frosting which was ao delicious. I highly recommend this place.

[2022-08-13] One day I passed by this cafe and remembered the name because I was surprised by what some customers were eating, and today I decided to go. I ordered this chicken bowl and it was really good, I definitely recommend it, plus the presentation is nice too.

[2022-01-19] A varied menu with healthy options, wraps, bowls, toasts... I tried the Heura Bowl and it was great. They have both vegan and non-vegan options. I love that the water comes in a carton and not a plastic bottle. A great bar philosophy. I'll be back.

[2020-10-26] We went for lunch and everything was delicious. The tofu bowl is amazing, and the protein balls are addictive ;) I also give the carrot cake and the coffee a 10/10. The service was excellent; you can tell they cook with love. We'll be back!!

[2021-02-07] My first delivery order from Maybean and everything was fantastic. Both Uber Eats' punctuality and the food itself were excellent. The bowl was delicious, and everything arrived in recyclable packaging. Top-notch.

[2020-10-03] I'm a lover of healthy food, and I stumbled upon this new place by chance. It has pleasantly surprised me; they have incredible dishes, the chicken bowl is highly recommended, and the prices are very good.

[2025-01-16] Cozy coffee shop with amazing tasting food. The salmon poke bowl was amazing, and came with an energy ball and a drink. Dedicated seating to work. Great music, energy, vibes. Very productive day. 🙂
[2025-01-17] The cafe was very cozy and a perfect place to chat and grab a bite. The tofu bowl was filled with fresh veggies and an AMAZING dressing. The price was reasonable for the quality!
[2025-07-30] I really enjoyed it! Healthy and delicious food. I ordered the lunch combo which came with the chicken bowl and the peanut butter and chocolate tart.
[2025-01-03] I had the Cardinal bowl. It was mostly yogurt with jam. There were literally 3 nuts. The fruit didn't taste fresh. It wasn't worth the price.
[2022-09-19] Stunning tofu bowl with roast vegetables in a teriyaki sauce. Very friendly and helpful service. Outdoor seating with shade and trees.
[2023-02-19] A very pleasant place, I loved the decor. We tried a Heura bowl, just as described, very tasty and fresh. We'll be back.

[2022-01-13] Simple, fresh, delicious, and vegan food! The Hummus Buddha Bowl is spectacular!
The coffee is a resounding 10.
[2022-10-09] Great food. We tried salmon bowl and banana toast. Bowl was awesome and would definitely order it again!
[2025-04-11] Friendly staff, delicious bowl, very good service when something went wrong with the order
[2025-02-08] We ordered the chicken wrap, salmon bowl and camel bowl. All were really good. Thank you
[2022-07-18] Cool place and good beer. We had the salmon bowl and hummus, it was ok. Rich portions 😊
[2022-07-03] Chicken salad bowl is amazing. Very friendly. Must go
[2023-07-16] Great chicken bowl, great cold brew!
